meta.landingDescription: Explore what carpentry in Saskatchewan is really like

salaryHook.hookQuestion: How much does a carpenter in Saskatchewan actually make?

salary.label: Median Annual Salary
salary.source: Government of Canada Job Bank, NOC 72310 — Regina Region
salary.seasonalityNote: 53% work year-round  ·  Average 41 weeks/year even for seasonal workers

taskRanking.heading: What do carpenters actually do?
taskRanking.subtext: Which of these tasks sound most interesting to you?
taskRanking.instruction: Drag to rank from most to least interesting

reveal.heading: Here's how your ranking compares
reveal.subtext: See how your interests line up with actual job time.

tiles.title: Framing
tiles.description: Build walls, floors, and roof structures — the skeleton of every building

tiles.title: Measuring & Layout
tiles.description: Read blueprints and use lasers and levels to lay out exactly where things go

tiles.title: Finishing Work
tiles.description: Install trim, cabinets, stairs, and flooring — the details everyone notices

tiles.title: Concrete & Foundations
tiles.description: Build forms, place rebar, and pour the concrete base everything stands on

tiles.title: Roofing
tiles.description: Install trusses, shingles, and insulation to handle Saskatchewan winters

tiles.title: Renovation
tiles.description: Carefully take apart old structures and rebuild them into something better

employerMap.heading: Who hires carpenters near you?
employerMap.subtext: Tap a pin to learn about real employers in the Regina area.

employers.name: PCL Construction
employers.description: One of the largest construction companies in North America. They build hospitals, schools, and major public buildings across Saskatchewan.
employers.quote: We handle billion-dollar projects — from hospitals to high-rises.

employers.name: Graham Construction
employers.description: An employee-owned company specializing in industrial projects like mining facilities, power plants, and large commercial buildings.
employers.quote: Every employee is an owner — that's what makes us different.

employers.name: Crawford Homes
employers.description: A Regina favourite since 1980, building energy-efficient custom homes and new subdivisions across the city.
employers.quote: We build homes families grow up in.

employers.name: Hipperson Construction
employers.description: Over 100 years of building Saskatchewan — from commercial buildings to civil infrastructure and public facilities.
employers.quote: We've been building this province for over a century.

employers.name: Schneider's Millwork
employers.description: A specialized woodworking shop since 1954. They create custom cabinets, reception desks, and fine wood finishes for homes and businesses.
employers.quote: Precision down to the millimetre — that's our standard.

employers.name: Alair Homes Regina
employers.description: A premium renovation and custom build firm focused on client transparency and high-quality craftsmanship.
employers.quote: Every project starts with listening to what you actually want.

employers.name: Fiorante Homes
employers.description: A family-driven builder since 1974, creating bespoke custom homes from foundations to final finishes.
employers.quote: Three generations of building homes people love.

employers.name: Munro Homes
employers.description: Award-winning luxury home builder known for architectural precision and premium craftsmanship in Regina.

employers.name: Ledcor Construction
employers.description: A diversified contractor working across building construction, civil engineering, and telecommunications infrastructure.
employers.quote: From buildings to bridges to telecom towers — we build it all.

employers.name: Winmar Property Restoration
employers.description: Disaster recovery specialists who rebuild homes and businesses damaged by fire, flood, and storms.
employers.quote: When disaster strikes, we rebuild what matters most.

careerPathway.heading: How do you become a carpenter?
careerPathway.subtext: Tap each step to see what comes next.

details.description: Take shop and PAA classes to build skills. Join the Saskatchewan Youth Apprenticeship (SYA) for a huge head start — earn 300 free hours, free Level 1 tuition, and a shot at a $1,000 scholarship.

details.earnings: Starts at 60% of journeyperson pay, rises to 90%
details.description: Work full-time alongside a certified carpenter and get paid while learning. Attend 7-8 weeks of classroom training per year at Sask Polytech.

programs: Saskatchewan Polytechnic (Moose Jaw, Prince Albert, Saskatoon)
details.description: A hands-on program covering blueprints, concrete, framing, finishing, and trade math. Includes a 2-week work placement with a real employer.
Pass the Red Seal exam — the gold standard in Canadian trades. Your certification is recognized in every province and territory, so you can work anywhere in Canada.
Your Red Seal counts as 2 years of university credit. Top up to a full Bachelor of Applied Management degree at Sask Polytech — from the job site to the boardroom.

speedRun.disclaimer: Values are illustrative estimates based on Saskatchewan averages and may vary.

aiSorting.heading:
Who is better
at each task?
aiSorting.subtext: Can AI do a carpenter's job?
aiSorting.punchline: AI can crunch numbers and draft plans — but it can't swing a hammer, read a crooked wall, or build something you can walk through. Carpenters are irreplaceable.

tasks.description: Calculate the lumber needed for a 2,400 sq ft house from a blueprint
tasks.explanation: AI can read specs and compute material takeoffs faster than any human.

tasks.description: Frame a wall plumb and square on an uneven concrete slab
tasks.explanation: Adjusting to real-world imperfections requires hands, eyes, and experience.

tasks.description: Generate a 3D model of a kitchen renovation from photos
tasks.explanation: AI vision tools can already produce 3D renders from smartphone photos.

tasks.description: Scribe and fit trim against a wavy plaster wall
tasks.explanation: Scribing requires feel, patience, and micro-adjustments no robot can replicate on site.

tasks.description: Schedule subcontractors and optimize a 6-month build timeline
tasks.explanation: Scheduling optimization is a classic AI strength — faster and fewer conflicts.

tasks.description: Safely demolish a load-bearing wall while supporting the structure above
tasks.explanation: Structural demo is high-stakes physical work — one wrong move and the roof comes down.

vrPrep.heading: Get ready for VR
vrPrep.subtext: Keep these questions in mind while you explore.

prompts.text: What tools do you notice being used?
What safety gear and equipment can you spot?
Which task — framing, finishing, concrete — looks most interesting to you?

postVr.congratsSubtext: You just experienced what it's like to be a carpenter. Here are some next steps to keep the momentum going.

checklist.label: Search for "Carpenter" in Work > Occupations and favourite the career. Read through the occupation profile to learn about daily tasks, required skills, and salary.
Complete the Compatibility Survey for Carpenter, found within the Do I Match? section of the occupation profile.
Create a new portfolio called Carpentry Exploration.
Add the Carpenter occupation to your Carpentry Exploration Portfolio and write a reflection talking about one new thing you learned today.
Set a goal related to your exploration today. For example: I want to talk to a carpenter about what they like most about their job this summer. Add that goal to your Carpentry Exploration Portfolio.
Review the "I can" statements on the next page and check off all that feel true for you.

postVr.reflectionSubtext: Check off the statements that feel true for you after today's experience.

reflections.statement: I can describe what a carpenter does on a typical workday.
I can name at least two different types of tasks involved in carpentry (e.g., framing, finishing, blueprint reading).
I can identify a company or organization in my region that employs carpenters.
I can explain the steps I would need to take from high school to become a certified carpenter in Saskatchewan.
I can name a high school course I could take that connects to a career in carpentry.
I can describe something that surprised me about the VR carpentry experience.
I can explain why carpentry is considered an in-demand career in Saskatchewan right now.
I can identify one strength or interest of mine that connects to a career in carpentry.
